welcomeScreen.title: Bulk IOC Lookup
welcomeScreen.intro: This tool processes IOCs in batches, automatically categorizes them by type, and provides threat intelligence data from various security services.
welcomeScreen.instructions: Paste your IOCs (one per line) or upload a file to get started. The system will automatically detect IOC types and query relevant threat intelligence sources in parallel for fast analysis.

welcomeScreen.title: Single IOC Lookup
welcomeScreen.intro: Investigate various Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) using multiple threat intelligence sources like VirusTotal, AlienVault, AbuseIPDB, and more, to gain detailed insights into potential security threats.
welcomeScreen.description: The tool automatically identifies the IOC type and correlates data from relevant sources, enabling rapid threat assessment and informed security decision-making.

ipAddresses.title: IP Addresses
ipAddresses.description: IPv4 and IPv6 addresses for threat analysis

emailAddresses.description: Known malicious or suspicious email addresses

hashes.description: MD5, SHA1, and SHA256 file hashes

cryptoAddresses.title: Crypto Addresses
cryptoAddresses.description: EVM addresses (0x...) and Bitcoin addresses (legacy, P2SH, bech32)

welcomeScreen.addressReputationChecksIntro: Crypto addresses are screened against a self-hosted blacklist built from open, no-key data sources — no third-party API calls are made for this check.

ofacSdn.title: OFAC SDN (Sanctions)
ofacSdn.description: Checked against the U.S. Treasury's Specially Designated Nationals list, including digital currency addresses tied to sanctioned entities. Refreshed daily.

scamSniffer.title: ScamSniffer (Phishing)
scamSniffer.description: Checked against ScamSniffer's open-source blacklist of EVM addresses linked to known phishing scams. Refreshed daily.

welcomeScreen.title: Domain Finder
welcomeScreen.intro: Domain Finder is a module that helps you protect your organization from phishing attacks by allowing you to search for recently registered domains that match a specific pattern. This can help you identify potential threats before they occur.
welcomeScreen.description: Using the URLScan.io API, the module allows you to view screenshots of websites to see what is behind a domain without the need to visit the site and potentially expose yourself to danger. Additionally, with just a single click, you can check each domain and the IP it resolves to against multiple threat intelligence services to further protect your organization.
welcomeScreen.example: For example, you can use the module to search for domains that start with "google-" by using the search pattern "google-*".

patternMatching.title: Pattern Matching
patternMatching.description: Search for recently registered domains using specific patterns

safePreview.title: Safe Preview
safePreview.description: View website screenshots via URLScan.io integration

threatIntelligence.title: Threat Intelligence
threatIntelligence.description: Check domains and IPs against multiple security services

proactiveDefense.title: Proactive Defense
proactiveDefense.description: Identify potential phishing threats before they become active

defang.title: Defanging IOCs
defang.subtitle: Make IOCs safe for sharing
defang.description: Defanging replaces dangerous characters in IOCs to prevent accidental execution:

fang.subtitle: Restore original IOCs for analysis
fang.description: Fanging restores defanged IOCs to their original form for analysis:

welcomeScreen.title: IOC Defang/Fang Tool
welcomeScreen.subtitle: Supported Defanging Techniques
welcomeScreen.description: Safely defang IOCs for sharing or restore fanged IOCs for analysis. This tool automatically detects IOC types and applies fanging or defanging techniques.

welcomeScreen.title: IOC Extractor
welcomeScreen.intro: IOC Extractor is a module that allows you to extract and organize indicators of compromise (IOCs) from unstructured files using regular expressions (Regex). The module automatically removes any duplicates, so you don't have to worry about sorting through the same IOCs multiple times.
welcomeScreen.usage: Just drop your file containing the IOCs into the tool and let it do the work for you. With a single click, you can analyze every detected IOC, saving you the time and effort of building Excel sheets to extract IOCs from files manually.

automatedExtraction.title: Automated Extraction
automatedExtraction.description: Extracts IOCs from unstructured files using regular expressions

duplicateRemoval.title: Duplicate Removal
duplicateRemoval.description: Automatically removes duplicate IOCs from the results

simpleInterface.title: Simple Interface
simpleInterface.description: Drop files and get results with no configuration needed

oneClickAnalysis.title: One-Click Analysis
oneClickAnalysis.description: Analyze each detected IOC with a single click

crowdsec.notFound: IP not found in CrowdSec CTI.
crowdsec.insufficientData: Insufficient data received from CrowdSec CTI.
crowdsec.scoreInfoText:
The scores indicate malevolence for an IP over time (Overall, Last Day, Week, Month).

- Aggressiveness: Intensity of attacks (number reported).
- Threat Level: Seriousness of reported attack types (e.g., crawl vs. exploit).
- Trust: Confidence in reporting actors (reputation, diversity).
- Anomaly: Red flags from IP's static description.
- Total: Aggregation of these components (0=low to 5=high malevolence).

mandiant.noInfoFound: No detailed intelligence information found for this indicator in Mandiant.

nistnvd.noInfoFound: No detailed vulnerability information found for CVE ID: {{ioc}}.
nistnvd.notFound: CVE ID: {{ioc}} was not found in the NIST NVD.
nistnvd.referencesDisclaimer: References to advisories, solutions, and tools. By selecting these links, you may be leaving NIST webspace. NIST does not necessarily endorse the views expressed or products mentioned on these sites.
nistnvd.configurationsDisclaimer: CVE applicability statements conveying which product(s) are associated with the vulnerability.
nistnvd.vendorCommentsDisclaimer: Official Vendor Comment for the CVE. NVD provides a service whereby organizations can submit Official Vendor Comments for CVE associated with their products. Organizations can use the service in a variety of ways. For example, they can provide configuration and remediation guidance, clarify vulnerability applicability, provide deeper vulnerability analysis, dispute third party vulnerability information, and explain vulnerability impact. Official Vendor Comments can be submitted to the NVD by email at nvd@nist.gov.
nistnvd.weaknessesDisclaimer: This object contains information on specific weaknesses, considered the cause of the vulnerability. Source identifies the organization that provided the weakness information and type identifies whether the organization is a primary or secondary source. Primary sources include the NVD and CNA who have reached the provider level in CVMAP. 10% of provider level submissions are audited by the NVD. If a submission has been audited the NVD will appear as the primary source and the provider level CNA will appear as the secondary source.
